rationale: In the theory development, the citing paper uses Zhao, Hoeffler, and Zauberman (2011) as direct evidence that mental simulation can improve product evaluations and purchase intentions. That claim is then used to motivate key mediation hypotheses (especially H5, and relatedly H7) about why the CvSS architecture should increase satisfaction and feature-rich choices. The citation therefore does more than provide generic background; it supports a central mechanism in the paper's conceptual model. The citing paper also frames its contribution as adding to the mental simulation literature that includes Zhao et al.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Zhao et al. (2011) as conceptual and empirical groundwork for the proposition that mental simulation positively affects product evaluation. It builds on that foundation by testing a new antecedent (customization architecture) and downstream effects in product customization contexts.
